title: Indulgent Chewy Syrian Bulgur Pilaf
description: A rich, chewy pilaf that blends the nutty flavor of bulgur with a subtle tang from white vinegar and the umami of shio-koji, perfect for a kid-friendly dinner.
instructions: Rinse the bulgur under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat and sauté the onion until translucent.
Add the diced carrot and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the bulgur, white vinegar, and shio-koji cure, ensuring the grains are coated with the liquid.
Pour in the chicken broth, add salt and pepper, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 15 minutes or until the bulgur is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Fold in the green peas during the last 3 minutes of cooking.
Remove the pot from the heat and let the pilaf sit, covered, for 5 minutes to finish steaming.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork, taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve warm, optionally garnished with chopped parsley or a squeeze of lemon for extra brightness.
